what_it_does: A stdlib HTTP bridge that serves the operator UI console and API over the shared runtime Suite. It handles GET/POST routes, serves mockups and design-system CSS, manages mockup feedback JSONL threads, runs voice/chat streaming with brain-TTS overlap, exposes a gated interactive MCP tool face, wraps cognition helpers, and hosts several background daemon loops (vector-cache warm, commit-queue drain, freshness reindex).
observations: BRIDGE_ROUTES is declared as the single source of truth for all handled paths; Suite.capabilities() projects the /api/* subset from it, and a drift test is supposed to keep the literal dispatcher strings in sync.
The file is heavily annotated with dated contract references, gap numbers, and explicit safety/rationale comments.
Mockup serving and feedback use path-safe basename validation with realpath containment checks.
The corpus gallery is built from disk truth joined to a declared JSON meta registry, with ungrouped fallback.
Voice streaming uses a producer-consumer pattern: a daemon thread pulls chat_parts() ahead while the caller synthesizes sentences, aiming for brain-TTS overlap and cancellation gating.
Tool invocation has a fail-closed operator gate that mirrors remote_exposure.json, supports operator-only and hazard postures, and reserves an operator-token attribution slot.
Operator-session tokens are described as a safety (runaway-prevention) boundary, not an adversary-proof security boundary, and enforcement is flag-gated off.
Background daemons are started as daemon threads that swallow exceptions to avoid breaking startup.
Several projection endpoints (semantic, separator, nucleation) and a build_projection helper are present.
Cognition helpers (cog_run_role, cog_run_items, cog_run_reduce) wrap the shared cognition module for the bridge.
standards: BRIDGE_ROUTES as a self-documenting, drift-guarded route registry that also feeds capabilities().
The _stream_parts producer/consumer design for overlapping LLM generation and TTS synthesis.
The _tool_gate fail-closed operator invoke gate with posture, denylist, confirm, and attribution slots.
The explicit, honest scaffolding around operator tokens and what it does/does not protect against.
conventions: Registry-is-truth and no-hardcoding: corpus meta, tool meta, and exposure registries are loaded from JSON files rather than inline dicts.
Fail-loud and degrade-clean: missing security registries raise, missing presentation registries return empty.
Lazy imports to avoid circular/module-load issues (e.g., mcp_face.server imported only inside _tool_manager).
Daemon threads for background work with broad exception swallowing.
Nested helper functions inside methods for streaming closures (emit, client_gone, split, speak, tee).
concerns: The file is very large and mixes many responsibilities: HTTP server, static file serving, API routing, voice streaming, tool gating, projection math, cognition wrappers, and background daemons.
Global mutable state is used for the shared SUITE, operator token set, and cached tool manager.
Nested functions and closures inside handler methods make unit testing harder unless factored paths like _stream_parts are used.
Background daemon loops silently swallow all exceptions, which could hide persistent failures.
The provided file content is truncated, so the bodies of many POST handlers and some helpers are not visible and cannot be verified from this excerpt alone.
notes: The operator HTTP bridge: a monolithic stdlib server wiring UI routes, voice streaming, mockup review, tool invocation gating, and background maintenance over one shared Suite.
questions: What is the full implementation of do_GET and do_POST for the many routes listed in BRIDGE_ROUTES?
How does /api/tools/invoke actually dispatch to the underlying tool after _tool_gate returns allowed?
What is the exact contract and output shape of the projection endpoints (semantic, separator, nucleation, build_projection)?
How are the operator-session token minting and header validation wired into the actual POST routes?
What tests exercise the drift gate between BRIDGE_ROUTES and the dispatcher literals?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: it is a thin HTTP UI face over the shared Suite, serving pages and API routes while reusing the same brain and store as the MCP face.
intention_signals: To create a unified, local, human-operated surface over the same runtime system that the MCP agent uses, with explicit contracts, review workflows, and safety scaffolding that prevent accidental autonomous writes while still enabling rich interactive control.
novelty: Explicit drift-guarded BRIDGE_ROUTES that project into capabilities; a factored voice streaming core with producer-thread overlap; and an operator tool-invoke gate that mirrors the remote exposure posture registry with operator-specific deltas.
contribution: A self-contained stdlib HTTP bridge consolidating UI serving, API dispatch, mockup feedback, voice/chat streaming, tool gating, cognition wrappers, and background housekeeping, with extensive inline contract documentation and safety scaffolding.
summary_for_embedding: A stdlib HTTP request handler and route table for the operator UI bridge, binding a shared Suite to served pages, REST-ish API endpoints, mockup feedback, voice streaming, tool invocation gating, and background daemon loops.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the local human operator console over the same runtime brain as the MCP agent face, with an explicit route registry, fail-closed safeguards, and review-oriented workflows.

symbols.does: Resolves a mockup filename to a safe absolute path under MOCKUPS_DIR.
symbols.description: Validates that the supplied name is a bare .html basename, then joins it under the mockups directory and checks that the realpath stays inside the mockups dir. Raises ValueError on traversal or bad names.

symbols.does: Returns the JSONL feedback path for a mockup, path-safe.
symbols.description: Validates the mockup basename the same way as _safe_mockup_path and resolves the corresponding .jsonl file under FEEDBACK_DIR, refusing directory traversal.

symbols.does: Reads a mockup's feedback thread as a list of entries.
symbols.description: Opens the feedback JSONL if it exists, parses each line, skips malformed lines with a stderr warning, and returns oldest-first entries.

symbols.does: Builds the studio gallery corpus from disk plus declared meta.
symbols.description: Lists actual .html mockups under MOCKUPS_DIR (excluding STUDIO.html), joins each to curated metadata from _CORPUS_META, and provides derived defaults for uncurated files. Returns a sorted list of corpus records.

symbols.does: Sets a config-model's context window and auto-sizes GPU utilization.
symbols.description: Updates max_model_len for a registry service, computes gpu_util from a measured profile if available, and optionally restarts the service only when VRAM budget allows. Returns a status dict.

symbols.does: Finds the registry key of the active local GPU brain model.
symbols.description: Given a registry and runtime config, returns the service key whose group is 'brain' and whose configured model matches the rhm_config model, or None for cloud/ollama brains.

symbols.does: Maps a TTS engine name to its service base URL.
symbols.description: Returns the env-overridable TTS_URL for kokoro or no engine, returns 127.0.0.1:<port> for known alternate engines, and raises ValueError for unknown engines rather than falling back silently.

symbols.does: Drives a chat_parts generator with brain-TTS overlap via a producer thread.
symbols.description: Runs the generator in a daemon producer thread feeding a queue, while the caller synthesizes each completed part's sentences. Supports proactive disconnect probing, per-part callbacks, optional speakable cleaning, and cancellation gating. Returns reply metadata and re-raises brain errors.

symbols.does: Producer thread body that pulls parts from the generator.
symbols.description: Checks cancellation before generating the next part, puts parts on the queue, captures the final part, records any brain exception, and closes the generator when done.

symbols.does: Warms the vector-index cache in the background.
symbols.description: Calls SUITE.store.warm_vector_cache() on a daemon thread at startup, isolated from failures, so the first semantic query is not cold. Intentionally does not prewarm theorem claims.

symbols.does: Drains the shared commit queue periodically.
symbols.description: Runs an infinite loop calling ops.commit_queue.drain() every two seconds on a daemon thread, swallowing transient errors to keep the serializer alive.

symbols.does: Auto-reindexes extractions when source asset files change.
symbols.description: Polls mtime of extraction asset files every ten minutes; on change, reconciles the extractions index, emits a durable event if anything changed, and swallows transient errors.

symbols.does: Computes an mtime signature for extraction asset files.
symbols.description: Nested helper that returns a dict of asset names to st_mtime_ns values for the known extraction JSONL files.

symbols.does: Mints a per-process operator-session token.
symbols.description: Generates a URL-safe random token, stores it in the process-local set, and returns it. Intended as a runaway-prevention boundary, not cryptographic proof.

symbols.does: Checks whether a token was minted by this process.
symbols.description: Returns True if the supplied token is present in the process-local _OPERATOR_TOKENS set.

symbols.does: Lazily builds and caches the FastMCP tool manager bound to this bridge's Suite.
symbols.description: Imports mcp_face.server on first use, calls build_mcp(SUITE), and caches the resulting tool manager so no second Suite is constructed.

symbols.does: Loads the lead-owned tool presentation registry.
symbols.description: Reads mcp_face/tool_meta.json fresh per call and returns the tools map excluding underscore-prefixed keys; degrades to {} on missing or malformed files.

symbols.does: Loads the remote exposure posture registry.
symbols.description: Reads mcp_face/remote_exposure.json fresh per call and fails loud if it is missing or malformed, because the allow-list is the security boundary.

symbols.does: Loads the operator-face posture overlay.
symbols.description: Reads mcp_face/tool_operator_overlay.json and returns the tools map; degrades to {} if absent or malformed, providing a home for operator-only deltas.

symbols.does: Determines the effective operator-face posture for a tool.
symbols.description: Prefers the operator overlay's operator_posture, falls back to remote_exposure.json's remote_posture, and returns empty string if neither is present.

symbols.does: Fail-closed gate for generic tool invocation on the operator face.
symbols.description: Checks the explicitly_denied denylist, deny-by-default posture, operator posture gate (safe/design/locked/hazard with confirm), allowed-op whitelist, and reserves an operator-token attribution slot for consequential writes. Returns (allowed, reason, posture).

symbols.does: Classifies whether a tool posture represents a consequential write.
symbols.description: Returns True for postures that should require genuine-operator attribution, such as locked or hazard, and False for safe/design reads.

symbols.does: Builds a semantic projection query result.
symbols.description: Computes meaning-field coordinates for a query against a binding/registry/events/center, attaches scale rungs, centers the vector, and enriches the result body. Contains nested helpers _mrl, attach_scale, centre_vector, and enrich.

symbols.does: Nested helper for vector normalization in semantic projection.
symbols.description: Normalizes a vector, likely using Matryoshka Representation Learning truncation or L2 normalization.

symbols.does: Attaches scale/rung metadata to a projection body.
symbols.description: Nested helper that enriches the projection body with nested rung centroids for the meaning-field's ?rung= resolution.

symbols.does: Centers a vector around a reference point.
symbols.description: Nested helper that recenters a vector, probably subtracting a centroid or mean for relative projection.

symbols.does: Enriches a projection body with additional metadata.
symbols.description: Nested helper that adds derived fields to the projection result before returning it.

symbols.does: Builds a separator-style projection query result.
symbols.description: Computes projection coordinates using separator/pole vectors rather than semantic similarity. Contains nested helpers _mrl and _pole_vector.

symbols.does: Nested normalization helper for separator projection.
symbols.description: Same pattern as the semantic _mrl: normalizes or truncates a vector for the separator projection.

symbols.does: Computes a pole vector for separator projection.
symbols.description: Nested helper that resolves a reference into a pole vector used to define a separator axis.

symbols.does: Builds a nucleation-style projection query result.
symbols.description: Computes projection coordinates based on nucleation/clustering semantics, using cosine similarity. Contains nested helpers _mrl and _cos.

symbols.does: Nested normalization helper for nucleation projection.
symbols.description: Normalizes or truncates vectors for the nucleation projection.

symbols.does: Computes cosine similarity for nucleation projection.
symbols.description: Nested helper that returns the cosine between two vectors.

symbols.does: Top-level dispatcher that builds a projection response.
symbols.description: Selects among semantic, separator, and nucleation projection strategies based on the query and returns the assembled projection body.

symbols.does: Emits a cognition event to the store or event stream.
symbols.description: Helper that records a cognition event of a given kind with the supplied payload, used by the cognition wrappers.

symbols.does: Resolves a role identifier or fields into a role spec.
symbols.description: Looks up or normalizes role input so downstream cognition functions receive a consistent role definition.

symbols.does: Generates a unique turn identifier.
symbols.description: Creates a prefixed unique id string for cognition turns, used for tracing and event attribution.

symbols.does: Runs a single cognition role turn.
symbols.description: Wraps the shared cognition engine's run_role with bridge-level defaults, event emission, and optional ensure/coordinate parameters.

symbols.does: Runs cognition over a list of items for a role.
symbols.description: Wraps the shared cognition engine's run_items, applying max_tokens and temperature defaults.

symbols.does: Runs a cognition reduce operation over addresses.
symbols.description: Wraps the shared cognition engine's run_reduce, supporting modes, reduce rules, clustering thresholds, and token limits.

symbols.does: Seeds demo data into the system.
symbols.description: Initializes a demo dataset (DEMO = 'codebase') into the shared store/Suite for onboarding or testing.

symbols.does: HTTP request handler class for the bridge.
symbols.description: Subclass of BaseHTTPRequestHandler that dispatches GET and POST routes, serves static files and pages, streams chat/voice, and handles API calls.

symbols.does: Sends an HTTP response with a given status, body, and content type.
symbols.description: Utility method that writes the response code, headers, and body for both JSON and binary responses.

symbols.does: Reads the request body.
symbols.description: Utility method that returns the raw bytes or string of the current POST/PUT request body.

symbols.does: Parses the query string into a dict.
symbols.description: Utility method that takes a parsed URL and returns a parse_qs mapping, likely with single-value flattening.

symbols.does: Dispatches all GET routes.
symbols.description: The main GET handler that matches self.path against BRIDGE_ROUTES literals, serves pages, static files, mockups, streaming, corpus, graph, cognition, tool list, projections, and many read APIs.

symbols.does: Nested helper checking whether a decision is decided.
symbols.description: Used inside do_GET to test if a given decision id and address have reached a decided state.

symbols.does: Nested helper probing a service or endpoint.
symbols.description: Used inside do_GET to health-check or probe a named base endpoint and report status.

symbols.does: Generic streaming response helper.
symbols.description: Sets up an NDJSON or chunked streaming response over a queue, used by chat/voice/claude streaming routes.

symbols.does: Handles the chat streaming route.
symbols.description: Binds the shared Suite chat_parts generator to _stream_parts, emits NDJSON events per sentence/part, and returns the assembled reply metadata.

symbols.does: Nested event emitter for chat streaming.
symbols.description: Writes a single NDJSON event to the response stream inside _chat_stream.

symbols.does: Nested proactive disconnect probe for chat streaming.
symbols.description: Uses select/MSG_PEEK to detect whether the HTTP client has disconnected so generation can stop early.

symbols.does: Nested sentence splitter for chat streaming.
symbols.description: Splits a part's text into sentences for per-sentence TTS or display emission.

symbols.does: Nested generator tee for chat streaming.
symbols.description: Duplicates or fans out the chat_parts generator so display and TTS consumers can share the same stream.

symbols.does: Nested per-part display emitter for chat streaming.
symbols.description: Emits a {type:part} event carrying the raw part text to the client.

symbols.does: Handles the Claude Code side-panel streaming route.
symbols.description: Streams one turn of an embedded Claude Code session to the operator surface, with its own emit and client_gone closures.

symbols.does: Nested event emitter for Claude streaming.
symbols.description: Writes a single NDJSON event for the Claude side-panel stream.

symbols.does: Nested disconnect probe for Claude streaming.
symbols.description: Detects client disconnection during a Claude Code streaming turn.

symbols.does: Handles the voice streaming route.
symbols.description: Binds voice synthesis to _stream_parts, emits audio chunks as NDJSON events, and supports engine/voice selection and speakable cleaning.

symbols.does: Nested event emitter for voice streaming.
symbols.description: Writes a single NDJSON audio or status event during voice streaming.

symbols.does: Nested disconnect probe for voice streaming.
symbols.description: Detects client disconnection during voice streaming so synthesis can stop.

symbols.does: Nested sentence splitter for voice streaming.
symbols.description: Splits cleaned part text into sentences to be synthesized individually.

symbols.does: Nested TTS caller for voice streaming.
symbols.description: Synthesizes one sentence via the selected engine and returns wav bytes.

symbols.does: Nested audio-chunk emitter for voice streaming.
symbols.description: Emits a synthesized audio chunk with its index and text to the client.

symbols.does: Nested per-part display emitter for voice streaming.
symbols.description: Emits a {type:part} display event with raw part text during voice streaming.

symbols.does: Dispatches all POST routes.
symbols.description: The main POST handler that matches paths against BRIDGE_ROUTES literals and handles writes, model config, voice, mockup generation, cognition, tool invocation, decisions, journeys, trials, and activation ticks.

symbols.does: Logs an HTTP access line.
symbols.description: Override of BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message, likely writing to stderr with a custom prefix or filtering.
